meta.title: DBX-DEP-4: Governance, Federation and Cost Optimization

questions.stem: A team wants a Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines component that continuously ingests new files from cloud object storage and keeps a target table updated incrementally. Which approach is the BEST fit?

options.text: Use Auto Loader inside a pipeline flow or streaming table definition
Run a full `COPY INTO` from a notebook every hour
Create a materialized view over files in object storage without ingestion logic
Use a deep clone of the landing folder into bronze

questions.explanation: Auto Loader is designed for incremental file discovery and ingestion and is a common building block in Lakeflow pipelines. `COPY INTO` can be incremental, but it is not the best fit for an always-on managed pipeline pattern. Materialized views do not replace ingestion from raw files, and cloning folders is not an ingestion technique.
questions.reference: Databricks Data Engineer Professional exam guide; Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines concepts; Auto Loader documentation

questions.stem: A data engineer is packaging Python code for reuse across multiple Databricks jobs in different environments. Which project layout MOST directly supports Databricks Asset Bundles and CI/CD workflows?

options.text: Keep all production logic in a single notebook and copy it between workspaces
Store source code in a modular Python package under version control and deploy resources with bundle configuration files
Upload wheel files manually to each cluster after every code change
Save notebooks in a personal workspace folder and schedule them from there

questions.explanation: Bundles are intended to pair version-controlled source code with declarative resource definitions so deployments can be validated and automated across targets. The other options rely on manual promotion or personal workspace state, which is harder to test and automate.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Declarative Automation Bundles overview and reference

questions.stem: In a Databricks bundle configuration, why would a team define separate `dev` and `prod` targets?

options.text: To force all jobs to use the same cluster policy
To deploy the same project with different workspace settings, identities, or resource parameters per environment
To allow notebooks to bypass source control in development
To make Unity Catalog optional in production only

questions.explanation: Bundle targets let teams vary host settings, variables, run identities, and deployment behavior by environment while keeping one project definition. They do not exist to bypass source control or change fundamental governance requirements.
questions.reference: Declarative Automation Bundles targets and run identity documentation

questions.stem: A Python ETL package needs fast local-style validation before deployment. Select TWO practices that align with Databricks guidance for testing pipeline code. (Select TWO)

options.text: Write transformation logic as reusable functions, such as `DataFrame.transform`, so it can be tested independently
Use `assertDataFrameEqual` or `assertSchemaEqual` in automated tests
Rely on visual notebook inspection instead of automated assertions
Disable CI for data pipelines because Spark jobs are integration-only

questions.explanation: Reusable transformation functions make logic easier to unit test, and Databricks explicitly documents dataframe and schema assertion helpers for pipeline validation. Manual notebook inspection is not a substitute for tests, and CI is useful for data projects as well.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; PySpark testing utilities documentation

questions.stem: A job task needs a third-party Python dependency that is not preinstalled on the compute. Which solution is MOST appropriate for a repeatable production deployment?

options.text: Install the package interactively on one cluster and rely on cluster reuse
Declare the dependency in the job or bundle configuration so the environment is recreated consistently
Paste the library source into every notebook that needs it
Ask each analyst to install the package manually in their own workspace

questions.explanation: Production jobs should declare dependencies so new job runs can recreate the same environment consistently. Interactive installation or copying code into notebooks creates drift and weakens reproducibility.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Databricks compute and library management documentation

questions.stem: A Lakeflow pipeline requires a branch: if a control table indicates a full rebuild, recreate a gold table; otherwise run only incremental logic. Which statement is MOST accurate?

options.text: This kind of control flow can be implemented with conditional operators in the pipeline logic
Lakeflow pipelines only support straight-line transformations with no conditional behavior
Conditional logic is supported only for dashboards, not pipelines
Control flow requires replacing the pipeline with Delta Sharing

questions.explanation: The current exam guide explicitly includes creating pipeline components that use control flow operators such as if/else and for/each. The other options incorrectly claim that conditional orchestration is unsupported or unrelated.
questions.reference: Databricks Data Engineer Professional exam guide

questions.stem: A streaming ETL notebook uses `foreachBatch` to merge micro-batch results into a Delta target. Why is `foreachBatch` commonly used in this pattern?

options.text: It lets each micro-batch be handled with batch operations such as `MERGE`
It converts every stream into continuous processing mode automatically
It avoids the need for checkpointing
It guarantees exactly-once delivery to any external sink without idempotent logic

questions.explanation: `foreachBatch` exposes each micro-batch as a batch DataFrame, which makes Delta `MERGE` and other batch APIs available. It does not eliminate checkpointing requirements or automatically provide exactly-once semantics for arbitrary external systems.
questions.reference: Databricks Delta Lake merge documentation; Structured Streaming documentation

questions.stem: Which statement about streaming tables and materialized views in Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines is CORRECT?

options.text: Streaming tables are intended for incremental streaming ingestion, while materialized views are batch flows that process new data and changes when possible
Materialized views can only be built from static CSV files
Streaming tables always rewrite their full contents on every update
There is no behavioral difference; the names are interchangeable

questions.explanation: Databricks distinguishes streaming tables from materialized views: streaming tables are for streaming semantics, whereas materialized views are batch flows that incrementally process new or changed upstream data when possible. The other statements are inaccurate.
questions.reference: Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines concepts

questions.stem: A team wants a MERGE operation to be safe to rerun for the same input batch. Select TWO design choices that improve idempotency. (Select TWO)

options.text: Filter the source to a deterministic batch boundary such as a batch ID or watermark checkpoint
Add match conditions so older source records do not overwrite newer target values
Delete the checkpoint before rerunning to guarantee a clean merge
Randomize source row order before every run

questions.explanation: Idempotent MERGE patterns rely on deterministic source selection and update conditions that avoid replaying stale changes. Deleting checkpoints is risky and unrelated to MERGE correctness, and row order randomization does not help.
questions.reference: Delta Lake merge documentation; Databricks exam guide

questions.stem: A notebook task regularly hits driver memory limits because it collects large result sets back to Python. Which change is MOST likely to help?

options.text: Keep processing distributed in Spark transformations instead of using `collect()` on large datasets
Increase the trigger interval of unrelated streaming jobs
Switch the table format from Delta to CSV
Move the notebook into a dashboard

questions.explanation: Collecting large datasets to the driver is a common cause of driver memory pressure. Keeping work distributed is the first improvement. The other options do not address the driver-side bottleneck.
questions.reference: Apache Spark and Databricks performance best practices

questions.stem: Which Databricks feature is purpose-built for scalable incremental ingestion of new files from cloud storage directories?

options.text: Auto Loader
Delta Sharing
Deep Clone
Query Profile

questions.explanation: Auto Loader is Databricks' managed file ingestion mechanism for discovering and processing new files incrementally. Delta Sharing is for data sharing, Deep Clone copies tables, and Query Profile is for query analysis.
questions.reference: Auto Loader documentation

questions.stem: A bronze pipeline must ingest JSON, CSV, and binary files from cloud storage while preserving raw payloads for later reprocessing. Which medallion-layer design is BEST?

options.text: Normalize every source into a single wide gold table immediately
Land source-specific bronze tables that retain raw or minimally processed records before downstream refinement
Skip bronze and write cleansed silver tables directly from object storage
Convert all files to dashboards first

questions.explanation: Bronze is typically used for raw or minimally processed ingestion so teams can preserve source fidelity and reprocess later if needed. Jumping directly to gold or skipping bronze reduces recoverability and lineage clarity.
questions.reference: Databricks medallion architecture guidance; exam guide

questions.stem: A Delta table is used as the source for an append-only downstream job that should process only newly added rows after each run. Which feature is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Delta Change Data Feed or structured incremental reads, depending on the workload
VACUUM with a shorter retention period
A shallow clone of the source table
A row filter on the source table

questions.explanation: Change Data Feed is designed to expose row-level changes between table versions, which is useful for incremental downstream processing. VACUUM removes old files, cloning copies tables, and row filters are for access control rather than incremental ingestion.
questions.reference: Delta change data feed documentation

questions.stem: A team is evaluating ingestion approaches for files already present in cloud storage. Select TWO true statements. (Select TWO)

options.text: `COPY INTO` is useful for incremental batch loading from files into Delta tables
Auto Loader is designed to scale file discovery and ingestion over time
Deep Clone is the recommended file ingestion engine for landing zones
Delta Sharing is required before Databricks can read cloud object storage

questions.explanation: `COPY INTO` supports incremental loading from files, while Auto Loader is Databricks' scalable ingestion mechanism for continuous or repeated file arrival. Deep Clone and Delta Sharing serve different purposes.
questions.reference: Auto Loader documentation; Databricks SQL `COPY INTO` documentation

questions.stem: A pipeline ingests late-arriving CDC extracts from cloud storage. Business users need every change applied in order to a target dimension table. Which pattern is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Use Lakeflow APPLY CHANGES logic or equivalent ordered CDC handling on Delta targets
Overwrite the entire target table for each file arrival
Run OPTIMIZE after every file and skip CDC sequencing
Load all files into separate views and let analysts reconcile them manually

questions.explanation: The exam guide explicitly calls out APPLY CHANGES APIs for simplifying CDC in Lakeflow pipelines. Ordered CDC logic is the right fit when late files must still be applied correctly. Full overwrites and manual reconciliation are weaker solutions.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Lakeflow APPLY CHANGES documentation

questions.stem: A silver transformation computes session-level metrics from event data. Which Spark operation is MOST appropriate when calculations depend on ordered events within each user?

options.text: Window functions partitioned by user and ordered by event timestamp
A cartesian join against the same table
`explode()` on all columns
`VACUUM` on the bronze table

questions.explanation: Window functions are built for calculations that depend on row order within a partition, such as sessionization, lag/lead, or running totals. The other choices do not solve ordered per-user calculations.
questions.reference: Spark SQL window functions documentation

questions.stem: A data quality rule should redirect malformed records to a separate quarantine location instead of silently discarding them. What is the PRIMARY benefit?

options.text: Analysts can recover and inspect invalid records without contaminating curated tables
Queries against valid records become impossible
It guarantees all future records are valid
It removes the need for schema definitions

questions.explanation: Quarantining bad data preserves problematic records for investigation and replay while protecting downstream silver and gold data. It does not eliminate the need for schema management or magically fix future records.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; data quality patterns with Lakeflow pipelines and Auto Loader

questions.stem: A pipeline performs a stream-stream join between orders and payments and must limit unbounded state growth. Select TWO actions that typically help. (Select TWO)

options.text: Define appropriate event-time watermarks
Constrain the join with a time range condition when applicable
Increase the number of dashboard viewers
Disable checkpointing to reduce metadata

questions.explanation: Watermarks and time-bounded join conditions help Spark evict old state in stream-stream joins. Dashboard viewers are unrelated, and disabling checkpointing harms reliability rather than controlling state.
questions.reference: Structured Streaming joins and watermark documentation

questions.stem: A batch transformation must add a column showing the previous order amount for each customer. Which PySpark function is the BEST fit?

options.text: `lag()` over a window partitioned by customer
`monotonically_increasing_id()`
`broadcast()`
`input_file_name()`

questions.explanation: `lag()` returns a prior row's value within a defined window and is the standard way to compare each row with the previous row in a customer partition. The other functions solve different problems.
questions.reference: Spark SQL window functions documentation

questions.stem: A left join between a very large fact table and a very small dimension table is unexpectedly expensive because Spark shuffles both sides. Which change is MOST likely to reduce cost?

options.text: Broadcast the small dimension table when it fits broadcast criteria
Convert the fact table to CSV before joining
Add more nullable columns to the dimension table
Run VACUUM on the target before the join

questions.explanation: Broadcasting a sufficiently small dimension avoids a full shuffle join by sending the small side to executors. Format changes or VACUUM do not directly fix the join strategy.
questions.reference: Spark SQL join optimization; Databricks query profile guidance

questions.stem: A multiple-select question in the mock exam repository must clearly indicate how many answers to choose. Select TWO stems that satisfy that requirement.

options.text: Select TWO actions that reduce streaming state growth.
Which actions reduce streaming state growth?
Select THREE statements that are true about Delta sharing.
Choose the best answer.

questions.explanation: For multi-select items, the stem should explicitly state the number of answers to select. The other stems are ambiguous and do not meet the repository rule.
questions.reference: Repository content rules from the generation prompt

questions.stem: A table has unexpected nulls in a required business key after ingestion. What is the BEST place to catch this before gold aggregations are built?

options.text: In silver-quality validation or expectation logic close to the cleansing step
Only after executives see a dashboard issue
During VACUUM retention checks
Inside Delta Sharing recipients

questions.explanation: Null business keys should be flagged as early as possible in cleansing and quality validation so downstream models do not amplify bad records. Waiting until reporting or unrelated maintenance steps is too late.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; medallion architecture and data quality practices

questions.stem: Which statement about Delta Sharing is CORRECT?

options.text: It can share live data to recipients without copying all data into their platform first
It is only available between notebooks in the same workspace
It requires all recipients to use the Databricks workspace UI
It replaces Unity Catalog permissions inside the provider account

questions.explanation: Delta Sharing is designed to share live data across platforms using the Databricks-to-Databricks or open sharing protocols. It does not require all recipients to use the same workspace UI, and it complements rather than replaces governance controls in the provider account.
questions.reference: Delta Sharing documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: A company wants Databricks users to query data that remains in an external database while governance is still managed through the lakehouse. Which capability BEST fits this requirement?

options.text: Lakehouse Federation
Auto Loader
Deep Clone
Change Data Feed

questions.explanation: Lakehouse Federation is for governed access to data that remains in supported external systems. Auto Loader ingests files, cloning copies Delta tables, and CDF exposes changes from Delta tables.
questions.reference: Lakehouse Federation documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: Select TWO scenarios where Delta Sharing is a better fit than creating a copied table in another workspace. (Select TWO)

options.text: A partner needs access to current shared data without managing repeated full copies
A provider wants to expose a governed subset of data to an external platform
A team needs to enforce Python package versions on a job cluster
An engineer wants to debug task logs from a failed run

questions.explanation: Delta Sharing is appropriate when you want live, governed data access across platforms or organizations without maintaining repeated data copies. Cluster package management and job-log debugging are unrelated.
questions.reference: Delta Sharing documentation

questions.stem: What is a key governance consideration when configuring Lakehouse Federation?

options.text: Ensure access to the external source is granted and governed appropriately through supported federation controls
Disable Unity Catalog so source-system permissions are the only control layer
Use federation only for CSV files in DBFS
Assume all pushdown behavior is identical across every external source

questions.explanation: Federation still requires correct source access and governance setup, typically coordinated with Unity Catalog and the supported connector behavior. The other statements are oversimplified or false.
questions.reference: Lakehouse Federation documentation

questions.stem: Which Databricks feature gives engineers a built-in record of pipeline execution details for Lakeflow pipelines?

options.text: Pipeline event logs
Deep clone history
Delta Sharing recipient tokens
Workspace browser history

questions.explanation: Lakeflow pipelines expose event logs that are useful for understanding updates, failures, and pipeline behavior. The other choices do not serve as primary pipeline observability records.
questions.reference: Lakeflow pipeline event log documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: A team wants to analyze resource utilization, workload history, and cost observability using built-in account data. Which source is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Databricks system tables
Notebook revision history
Cluster init scripts
Secret scopes

questions.explanation: System tables are the built-in observability source for areas such as jobs, billing, audit, and workload monitoring. Notebook history, init scripts, and secrets are not account-wide observability tables.
questions.reference: Databricks system tables documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: When should a data engineer use Query Profile or Spark UI?

options.text: To inspect query plans, shuffles, skew, and stage-level bottlenecks
To rotate GitHub tokens
To manage Delta Sharing recipients
To rename Unity Catalog schemas

questions.explanation: Query Profile and Spark UI are performance and execution diagnostics tools. They are not used for credential rotation, recipient management, or object renaming.
questions.reference: Databricks query profile and Spark UI documentation

questions.stem: A production team needs notifications when a scheduled job fails or runs longer than expected. Select TWO good approaches. (Select TWO)

options.text: Configure job notifications in Lakeflow Jobs
Use jobs APIs or CLI to inspect run state programmatically
Wait for users to report stale dashboards
Disable retries so no alerts are needed

questions.explanation: Databricks supports notifications in the Jobs UI and also exposes run-state monitoring through APIs and the CLI. Waiting for user reports is reactive, and disabling retries does not eliminate the need for alerting.
questions.reference: Lakeflow Jobs UI/API documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: A data quality threshold should trigger an operational alert when the daily null-rate exceeds 2%. Which Databricks mechanism is MOST directly suited to this?

options.text: A SQL alert on a quality-monitoring query
A deep clone of the quality table
A secret scope for null-rate metadata
A Unity Catalog storage credential

questions.explanation: SQL alerts are designed to notify based on query results, including data-quality thresholds. Clones, secret scopes, and storage credentials are unrelated to threshold-based alerting.
questions.reference: Databricks SQL alerts documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: Why do Unity Catalog managed tables often reduce operational overhead compared with many externally managed table patterns?

options.text: Databricks manages more of the storage layout and lifecycle integration for managed tables
Managed tables cannot be optimized
Managed tables prevent all schema evolution
Managed tables eliminate the need for permissions

questions.explanation: The exam guide explicitly notes that Unity Catalog managed tables can reduce operations overhead and maintenance burden. They do not eliminate governance or optimization considerations.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Unity Catalog managed tables documentation

questions.stem: Which feature helps Delta tables update clustering layout over time without relying on static partition choices?

options.text: Liquid clustering
Notebook widgets
Delta Sharing
Workspace ACLs

questions.explanation: Liquid clustering is the Delta feature for flexible data layout optimization without fixed partitioning schemes. Widgets, sharing, and ACLs are unrelated.
questions.reference: Liquid clustering documentation

questions.stem: Select TWO performance techniques Databricks commonly uses to reduce unnecessary data reads on large Delta tables. (Select TWO)

options.text: Data skipping
File pruning
Printing the full dataframe to the driver
Creating more personal workspace folders

questions.explanation: Data skipping and file pruning both reduce unnecessary reads by narrowing which files or file regions must be scanned. Printing dataframes and creating workspace folders do not improve scan efficiency.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Delta performance documentation

questions.stem: A table with frequent row-level updates experiences write conflicts and performance issues. Which statement about deletion vectors is MOST accurate?

options.text: Deletion vectors can improve row-level operation behavior and interact with modern Delta optimization features such as liquid clustering
Deletion vectors are only for dashboards
Enabling deletion vectors disables all query optimizations
Deletion vectors replace checkpointing in Structured Streaming

questions.explanation: Databricks highlights deletion vectors as a Delta optimization feature and notes their relationship to row-level operations and clustering. They do not replace streaming checkpoints or apply only to dashboards.
questions.reference: Liquid clustering and Delta optimization documentation

questions.stem: An engineer notices a query spends most time in a large shuffle caused by a poorly chosen join strategy. What is the BEST first diagnostic tool?

options.text: Query Profile
Secret scope ACLs
Catalog Explorer tags
Delta Sharing tokens

questions.explanation: Query Profile is specifically meant to reveal physical plan details and bottlenecks such as shuffles or inefficient joins. The other options are governance features, not diagnostics tools.
questions.reference: Databricks query profile documentation

questions.stem: A team wants lower-latency downstream processing from a Delta source used by a streaming table. Select TWO true statements about Change Data Feed (CDF). (Select TWO)

options.text: CDF records row-level change events between table versions
CDF can be useful when only changed rows should be processed downstream
CDF is the same as Delta Sharing recipient access
Cloning a table preserves the exact same CDF history as the source

questions.explanation: CDF captures inserts, updates, and deletes between Delta versions and is useful for incremental downstream processing. It is not the same as Delta Sharing, and cloned tables have separate history.
questions.reference: Delta change data feed documentation

questions.stem: A table owner wants different regions to see only their own rows at query time. Which feature is MOST directly designed for this?

options.text: A row filter policy
A broadcast join hint
A deep clone
An OPTIMIZE command

questions.explanation: Row filters restrict which rows users can see at query time and are the standard mechanism for row-level security in Unity Catalog. The other options are unrelated.
questions.reference: Row filters and column masks documentation

questions.stem: Which statement about column masks is CORRECT?

options.text: They can transform or redact values returned from a protected column at query time
They permanently overwrite the underlying stored data
They only work on dashboard titles
They replace all table permissions

questions.explanation: Column masks modify what a querying user sees for a column without rewriting base data. They complement permissions rather than replace all access control.
questions.reference: Row filters and column masks documentation

questions.stem: Select TWO examples of privacy-preserving techniques explicitly aligned with the exam guide's security objectives. (Select TWO)

options.text: Hashing sensitive identifiers
Tokenization of confidential fields
Broadcasting every join
Using only personal compute clusters

questions.explanation: The exam guide explicitly references anonymization and pseudonymization methods such as hashing and tokenization. Broadcast joins and personal clusters are unrelated to privacy controls.
questions.reference: Databricks Data Engineer Professional exam guide

questions.stem: A team stores PII in a Delta table and must keep the raw values for tightly controlled users while showing redacted values to most analysts. What is the BEST approach?

options.text: Apply governance controls such as column masks or a dynamic-view-style pattern rather than creating multiple inconsistent copies
Export two CSV files with different schemas every day
Disable Unity Catalog on the table
Use VACUUM after each query

questions.explanation: Fine-grained governance controls allow one governed source of truth while redacting sensitive columns for broader audiences. Creating duplicate exports increases drift and governance risk.
questions.reference: Row filters and column masks documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: A compliance policy requires deleting personal data after a retention period. Which data engineering responsibility BEST aligns with this requirement?

options.text: Develop a data purging solution that enforces retention policies across the relevant pipeline and storage layers
Increase shuffle partitions
Add extra notebook comments
Turn every table into a materialized view

questions.explanation: The exam guide explicitly calls out developing data purging solutions to meet retention policies. The other options do not address compliance deletion obligations.
questions.reference: Databricks Data Engineer Professional exam guide

questions.stem: Which Unity Catalog behavior is important when designing privileges across catalog, schema, and table objects?

options.text: Permissions follow an inheritance model that engineers should understand when granting access
Privileges only exist at the individual column level
All access must be granted separately on every object with no hierarchy
Unity Catalog cannot store metadata descriptions

questions.explanation: The exam guide explicitly includes understanding Unity Catalog permission inheritance. The other options ignore the hierarchical governance model and metadata features.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Unity Catalog privileges documentation

questions.stem: Why should data engineers add descriptions and other metadata to enterprise data assets?

options.text: To improve discoverability and understanding of shared data
To increase shuffle parallelism
To disable lineage capture
To bypass ACLs

questions.explanation: The exam guide calls out adding descriptions and metadata so enterprise data is easier to discover and use correctly. Metadata does not replace governance or affect shuffle parallelism.
questions.reference: Databricks Data Engineer Professional exam guide; Unity Catalog metadata documentation

questions.stem: Select TWO governance outcomes supported by a well-managed Unity Catalog implementation. (Select TWO)

options.text: Centralized discovery of governed data assets
Consistent permission management across securable objects
Automatic elimination of all bad data
Guaranteed zero compute cost

questions.explanation: Unity Catalog helps centralize discovery and governance across data assets. It does not automatically fix data quality or eliminate compute costs.
questions.reference: Unity Catalog documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: A scheduled job run failed because one task used the wrong parameter value. Which Databricks capability is MOST relevant for rerunning only the failed work with adjustments?

options.text: Job repair with parameter overrides
Delta Sharing recipient rotation
Deep clone rollback
Storage credential recreation

questions.explanation: The exam guide explicitly includes remediating failed job runs with repairs and parameter overrides. The other options address unrelated administration tasks.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; Lakeflow Jobs documentation

questions.stem: An engineer needs to identify why a Spark stage failed due to skew and spilling. Which source should they inspect FIRST?

options.text: Spark UI for stage, task, and shuffle diagnostics
Catalog Explorer descriptions
Delta Sharing profiles
Workspace bookmarks

questions.explanation: Spark UI is the primary source for stage execution details, skew indicators, task metrics, and spill diagnostics. The other options do not provide runtime execution details.
questions.reference: Spark UI documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: What is a key benefit of deploying Databricks resources through bundles instead of hand-configuring them in each workspace?

options.text: Deployments become versioned, repeatable, and easier to integrate with Git-based CI/CD
Bundles remove the need for testing
Bundles guarantee no runtime errors
Bundles eliminate all permissions management

questions.explanation: Bundles are designed for reproducible, version-controlled deployment workflows. They do not remove the need for testing, runtime validation, or governance.
questions.reference: Declarative Automation Bundles documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: Select TWO useful sources of diagnostic information when troubleshooting a failed production data pipeline. (Select TWO)

options.text: Pipeline event logs
System tables or job run metadata
Dashboard color themes
Workspace avatar settings

questions.explanation: Event logs and system/job metadata are directly useful for understanding failures, timings, and statuses. Visual personalization settings are irrelevant.
questions.reference: Databricks exam guide; system tables and Lakeflow event log documentation

questions.stem: A team stores notebooks and code in Databricks Git folders linked to a remote repository. What is the PRIMARY advantage?

options.text: Changes can participate in normal Git workflows such as branching, review, and CI/CD
All jobs become serverless automatically
Secrets no longer need ACLs
Unity Catalog is no longer required

questions.explanation: Git integration supports standard software development workflows for notebooks and code. It does not automatically alter compute mode, secret security, or catalog requirements.
questions.reference: Databricks Git folders and CI/CD guidance; exam guide

questions.stem: A bundle deploy succeeds in development but fails in production because the job must run as a service principal there. Which bundle capability is MOST relevant?

options.text: Environment-specific target configuration, including `run_as` identity settings
Broadcast hash join hints
Delta VACUUM retention changes
Column mask UDF arguments

questions.explanation: Bundles support environment-specific target settings, including run identities, which is exactly the sort of difference commonly needed between dev and prod. The other options are unrelated.
questions.reference: Declarative Automation Bundles run identity and targets documentation

questions.stem: A very large fact table is queried most often by date ranges such as a specific business day or month. Which column is usually the BEST partition candidate when cardinality is reasonable?

options.text: A date column commonly used in filters
A free-text comment column
A UUID-like transaction identifier with extremely high cardinality
A floating-point measure

questions.explanation: A date column often works well for partitioning because it aligns with common filters and has manageable cardinality. High-cardinality IDs and free-text fields are poor partition candidates.
questions.reference: Databricks sample exam guidance; Delta partitioning best practices

questions.stem: What is a major advantage of liquid clustering over rigid partitioning for some large analytical tables?

options.text: It allows data layout to evolve without locking the table into one static partition design
It makes schema enforcement unnecessary
It turns every query into a broadcast join
It can only be used on non-Delta tables

questions.explanation: Liquid clustering improves layout flexibility by reducing reliance on one static partition strategy. It does not remove schema enforcement and is a Delta capability.
questions.reference: Liquid clustering documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: A star-schema design is being built for BI workloads. Select TWO common characteristics of a fact table. (Select TWO)

options.text: Stores measurable events such as sales amounts or quantities
Contains foreign keys to related dimensions
Usually stores every business description as a nested JSON blob only
Exists only for streaming queries

questions.explanation: Fact tables typically store measurable events and link to descriptive dimensions through keys. They are not defined by nested JSON-only storage or by being streaming-only.
questions.reference: Dimensional modeling best practices; exam guide

questions.stem: A gold table supports many analyst queries that filter by customer segment today but may filter by geography or product family later. Which layout choice best preserves flexibility?

options.text: Prefer a design such as liquid clustering over over-partitioning by one current access pattern
Create a separate gold table per analyst
Hard-code a partition for every conceivable column
Store all dimensions in notebook widgets instead of tables

questions.explanation: When access patterns can evolve, liquid clustering generally preserves more flexibility than aggressive static partitioning on one dimension. The other options create sprawl or are nonsensical.
questions.reference: Liquid clustering documentation; exam guide

questions.stem: In a medallion architecture, which layer most commonly presents business-ready dimensional models and curated aggregates?

options.text: Gold
Bronze
Landing
Checkpoint

questions.explanation: Gold commonly contains business-ready models, curated dimensions, and aggregates for analytics. Bronze and landing are earlier/rawer layers, and checkpoints are streaming state artifacts.
questions.reference: Databricks medallion architecture guidance

questions.stem: Select TWO reasons a data engineer might avoid partitioning a Delta table by a very high-cardinality column. (Select TWO)

options.text: It can create many small partitions and files
It often weakens pruning benefits because values are too granular
It is required for Delta Change Data Feed
It improves every write workload automatically

questions.explanation: Very high-cardinality partition columns can create excessive small partitions and often do not provide practical pruning benefits. They are not required for CDF and do not automatically improve writes.
questions.reference: Delta Lake partitioning best practices

questions.stem: A dimension table needs a stable surrogate key while preserving the natural business key for matching incoming CDC updates. Which modeling approach is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Store both the surrogate key for analytics and the natural key for source-system reconciliation
Replace every natural key with a random dashboard title
Keep only measure columns in the dimension table
Use Delta Sharing instead of a key design

questions.explanation: Dimensional models often keep a surrogate key for analytics while retaining a natural key to support source-system integration and CDC reconciliation. The other options do not address the modeling requirement.
questions.reference: Dimensional modeling practices; exam guide

questions.stem: A team wants a copy of a production table for isolated experimentation without affecting the source table's governance and history. Which statement is MOST accurate?

options.text: A clone creates a separate table object; cloned tables do not share the exact same change history as the source
A clone is the same table, so all writes modify the original automatically
Cloning is only available for CSV files
A clone bypasses Unity Catalog permissions permanently

questions.explanation: Delta cloning creates a separate table object for experimentation or backup scenarios. Databricks notes that cloned tables have separate history, which also affects CDF expectations. Cloning does not bypass governance.
questions.reference: Delta clone and change data feed documentation
